stem: A mission enters the space mission pipeline. The probability of passing design review is 90%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing testing is between 58% and 77% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing launch is 85%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing orbital insertion is 94%. What is the overall probability that the mission is successful?

ground_truth.answer: P(successful) ranges from 0.417 to 0.554 as the testing pass rate varies from 58% to 77%. Point estimate: 0.489.

solution_steps: The mission must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the testing stage creates a 13.7 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the testing rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
